parentQuestion: The three basic regions of the cerebrum are the cerebral cortical gray matter, i...

scaffolds.q: The cerebrum is the largest part of the brain.
scaffolds.explain: The cerebrum dominates — 83% of total brain mass. That wrinkled dome of conscious thought dwarfs everything else. When people think 'brain,' they're picturing the cerebrum.

scaffolds.q: Gray matter contains neuron cell bodies, while white matter contains myelinated axons.
scaffolds.explain: Gray matter = neuron somas and dendrites, the computation hardware. White matter = myelinated axons, the data cables. The myelin lipids scatter light, making it appear white. Physics meets anatomy.
